channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's pick for education chief says Congress needed to shut down department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon told senators she believes it would take an act of Congress to abolish the Education Department, saying the Trump administration wants to “do this right” and craft a plan that senators will support.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Golden State Warriors blend sports and music with new album from their groundbreaking record label
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Golden State Warriors are known for their electrifying plays and superstar Stephen Curry, but now the team is pioneering a fresh gameplan: blending sports and entertainment in a way no NBA franchise has before.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Russia rejoices at Trump-Putin call as Zelenskyy rejects talks without Ukraine present
item.description: Russian officials and state media took a triumphant tone Thursday after President Donald Trump jettisoned three years of U.S. policy and announced he would likely meet soon with Russian President Vladimir Putin to negotiate a peace deal in the almost three-year war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: BARRY HATTON, ILLIA NOVIKOV and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump says he'll announce reciprocal tariffs on US trading partners on Thursday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday that he's announcing increases on U.S. tariffs to match the tax rates that other countries charge on imports. “TODAY IS THE BIG ONE: RECIPROCAL TARIFFS!!!” Trump posted on his social media site, Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Border czar Tom Homan meets with blue city leader for crackdown on criminal aliens
item.description: President Trump's border czar Tom Homan and New York CIty Eric Adams will meet to discuss cooperation between ICE and the NYPD on immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida man to be executed for 1997 double murder witnessed by toddler
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of murdering a husband and wife during a fishing trip at a remote farm while the couple's toddler looked on is scheduled to receive a lethal injection Thursday evening in the state's first execution this year.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Price fears for chocolatiers: Cocoa costs leave bitter taste for Valentine's Day
item.description: St. Valentine chocolates always seek to show how deep your love is. This year, it might just also show how deep your pockets are.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A free speech dispute over a New England town's pastry shop painting heads to trial
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A First Amendment dispute over a bright painting that shows sunbeams shining down on a mountain range made of sprinkle-covered chocolate and strawberry doughnuts, a blueberry muffin, a cinnamon roll and other pastries is scheduled for trial Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jury finds Megan Boswell guilty on all charges, including 3 counts of murder
item.description: SULLIVAN COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) — Jurors have returned a guilty verdict against Megan Boswell following a week-long trial. The jury found Boswell guilty on all three first-degree murder charges she faced in the death of her daughter Evelyn.
item.dc:creator: Murry Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Modi is meeting with Trump in a visit meant to boost the US-India relationship and avoid tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is meeting Thursday with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, who has heaped praise on him and is hoping to avoid tariffs that the new administration has slapped on other countries in its opening weeks.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court awaits Megan Boswell verdict from deliberating jury
item.description: SULLIVAN COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) - The jury in Megan Boswell’s trial will continue to deliberate Thursday. The jury was briefly called into the courtroom Thursday morning before being sent back to resume deliberating. On Wednesday, the prosecution and defense each gave their closing arguments.
item.dc:creator: Murry Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KREX 5

item.title: Live updates: Kennedy faces final Senate vote; Trump to meet Indian prime minister
item.description: Embattled nominee Robert F. Kennedy Jr. faces a final vote of the full Senate on Thursday, capping a twisting journey to become the nation's next Health and Human Services secretary.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Stars flee Kennedy Center groups after Trump seizes chair
item.description: President Donald Trump’s ascension to chair of the Kennedy Center on Wednesday led to multiple departures from the Washington, D.C., institution.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 28 are hurt as driver plows into a demonstration in Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring at least 28 people including children, authorities said. Authorities said it was believed to be an attack, and the suspect — an Afghan asylum-seeker — was arrested.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Could Canada really become the 51st US state? Here's what it would take
item.description: President Donald Trump has repeatedly said Canada should be the 51st U.S. state, erasing the 5,525-mile border the nations share and alleviating the need for tariffs he’s threatened against one of America’s top allies.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Californians brace for flooding in areas torched by wildfires as freezing rain, snow aim for Oregon
item.description: Rain has begun falling in California where an atmospheric river is expected to bring heavy downpours, winds and flooding to areas ravaged by wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley And John Raby, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: New York isn't alone when it comes to granting driver's licenses to immigrants without legal status
item.description: While U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi announced legal action Wednesday against New York officials over a 2019 state law allowing immigrants to obtain state-issued driver's licenses regardless of legal status, the Empire State isn't alone in having such a mandate on the books.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House says it has the right to punish AP reporters over Gulf naming dispute
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House said Wednesday that news organizations that refuse to use President Donald Trump’s new name for the Gulf of Mexico were telling “lies” and insisted it would continue to bar Associated Press journalists from presidential events.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The relationship between the White House and its press corps is time-tested
item.description: This week, the White House barred Associated Press journalists from three media appearances by President Donald Trump — two of them in the Oval Office itself.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: John Legend says family is considering leaving California after wildfires
item.description: Last month's devastating wildfires may prompt John Legend and his wife, Chrissy Teigen, the latest celebrities to flee California.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds singer R. Kelly's convictions, 30-year prison term
item.description: R. Kelly’s racketeering and sex trafficking convictions, along with a 30-year prison sentence, were upheld Wednesday by a federal appeals court that concluded the singer exploited his fame for over a quarter century to sexually abuse girls and young women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nation's Report Card: Which states have biggest drops in test scores
item.description: The Nation's Report Card, released by the NCES, compares fourth and eighth grade test scores in school districts around the country.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Could obesity drugs help with alcohol cravings?: new study
item.description: Medications that have transformed the treatment of obesity may also help people drink less alcohol, according to new government-funded research.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne announces 'final show' with original Black Sabbath
item.description: The performance, called "The Back to the Beginning," will cap off an all-day event featuring sets from several iconic heavy metal acts.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Navy fighter jet crashes into San Diego Bay near naval air station; 2 pilots rescued
item.description: Two military pilots were rescued after their Boeing EA-18G Growler jet crashed into the water in San Diego Bay on Wednesday morning. It's unclear what caused the Navy aircraft to crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: U.S. Navy fighter jet crashes near Shelter Island while attempting to land
item.description: A military plane crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island while attempting to land, according to the U.S. Navy.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: Alleged serial shoplifter banned from every Walmart in the USA
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A serial shoplifter has gotten herself banned from every Walmart in the United States of America due to her history of stealing from the company, Memphis Police say. Ashley Cross, 37, is charged with criminal trespass and theft of merchandise less than $1,000.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: List: The more than 500 JOANN stores expected to close amid second bankruptcy filing
item.description: When JOANN filed for bankruptcy last month, the company said it expected all of its stores to remain open.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baltimore sues Glock over handguns that can easily be converted to automatic weapons
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Baltimore is suing gunmaker Glock over design features that allow its handguns to be easily modified to become fully automatic weapons, which enables criminals to inflict more damage and makes the city more dangerous, attorneys argue in a complaint filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Government watchdogs fired by Trump sue his administration and ask a judge to reinstate them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Eight government watchdogs have sued over their mass firing that removed oversight of President Donald Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia school shooting suspect's father granted bond as victims share emotional statements in court
item.description: A Georgia judge granted Apalachee High School shooting suspect Colt Gray's father, Colin Gray, a $500,000 bond. Colin Gray has pleaded not guilty to 29 criminal counts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American teacher Marc Fogel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American history teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released and returned to the U.S. in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KTLA 5
